attributes.content:
## Overview

The Avatar card gives the workspace a structured home for user avatar/profile image with multiple sizes, fallback initials, and status indicator.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Avatar anchors identity, permissions, or relationship context. It becomes most useful when other cards can link to it consistently, query against it safely, and inherit its context without duplicating sensitive or fast-changing information.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Organization]]** -- Avatar frequently sits inside an account, company, or team-level relationship context.
- **[[Contact]]** -- Avatar often needs a person-level reference for ownership, communication, or accountability.
- **[[Task]]** -- Avatar often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Deal]]** -- Deal can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Avatar.
- **[[Case]]** -- Case can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Avatar.
- **[[Email Template]]** -- Email Template can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Avatar.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Avatar as the structured home for user avatar/profile image with multiple sizes, fallback initials, and status indicator and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Avatar when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Avatar stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.name: Avatar
cardInfo.summary: User avatar/profile image with multiple sizes, fallback initials, and status indicator.
